title: Cuisinart Single Serve Coffee Maker + Coffee Grinder, 48-Ounce Removable Reservoir, Black, DGB-2
description:
About this item
Note: 1)Too coarse a grind, too little coffee, or insufficiently tamping the grounds before brewing can all lead to inadequate pressure for a proper brew. 2)It is important to note that the amount of espresso extracted will vary depending on the grind size and amount and reprogramming may be needed when the size and amount are adjusted.Filter type:Reusable
SINGLE-SERVE: Enjoy three serving sizes with push button control – 8-ounces, 10-ounces or 12 ounces – compatible with any brand of single cup pod including Keurig K-Cup pods with an optional reusable filter cup so you can use your own coffee through our HomeBarista Cuisinart features
SUPERIOR FUNCTIONALITY: Our single-serve brewer has a 48-ounce removable water reservoir that complements its charcoal water filter and removable drip tray for travel mugs
GRIND YOUR BEANS: Our conical burr mill automatically grinds whole beans directly into the HomeBarista Reusable Filter Cup which is conveniently located in the storage compartment on the left side of the unit – The hopper holds 100-grams of beans and sealed lid keeps beans fresh
SIMPLIFY YOUR MORNING: Fill your tank with ease featuring our easy-view water window and brew a single serving with freshly ground beans or use your favorite pod
reviews_5s:
This is the best single-serve coffee maker I have ever had. I love that I get to use whole bean coffee, have it freshly ground and then brew it right away. It brews a full, generous 12 oz. cup of coffee. I had no idea with my Keurig I wasn't getting a full cup. While it's grinding, if I need to refill the water reservoir, it still grinds so it can multi-task. The brew basket has a thin piece of plastic for the hinge that already broke but I know I can replace the brew basket inexpensively. I can still use it. I just place the top on it before brewing. When it comes to refilling the coffee beans in the grinder, the only way I have found to do it is to spoon it in. That is a little tedious and sometimes the beans ricochet and go elsewhere but that's minor. After grinding the beans, I always tap the top of the grinder, the front of the grinder, and the brew basket because it tends to spill leftover ground coffee on the tray. It kind of works. Not foolproof. It seems there are always leftover grounds. I am very pleased with this coffee maker. It is so smartly engineered and makes a great cup of coffee!Great coffee maker, you have 2 choices, the pods or grind your own.
The reason there is a notation that this item is returned often, is because there is some work, cleaning and trowing out the coffee grind. ( I recycle the grinds on my flower bed,great fertilizer)
But the coffee is great, and a little bit of work is ok. You want fast coffee with no work, you have to go and buy it from one of the coffee establishment, but you will pay a small fortune for something this coffee maker can do for you.
If you are not afraid of some work, this coffee maker is great. Buy it you won't rgret it.So the first thing is you open it and it looks like the grinder is all dirty, so read the little paper that tells you they test it and clean it as best they can and be glad they test it. And you ignore that and try and clean it and then figure out they cleaned it as best as they can.

The grinder works well and just fills the included reusable cup to the right amount in about 10 to 15 seconds.
Brewing is a little interesting because unlike some other brewers it takes a min to heat up even after it's been turned on. It only heats the water right as you brew.

I ran into 1 problem with non standard k-cups that have a paper top. There's something with the way this one punctures and does pressure in a ring around the puncture point that made it so any of the paper topped K-Cups failed and grinds went everywhere.

Proper K-Cups work fine. But honestly it's just kind of nice to grind fresh beans but still have the option to use individual cups.

I think the downsides are that there's not really a great way to put big tumblers even if you took the drip tray away and mounted it up on something there's still the thing that holds the drip tray that's in the way you can kind of lean a Tumblr up to it but that's a thing to be aware of.

The water tank does not have LED lighting which is fine because it's behind it but it's something you get used to with some of the other ones.

The tank is pretty easy to lift up and pull it out so you can kind of take advantage of this being a smaller size Brewer and even the tank being in the back it's still something that easily can be lifted out and around the side of it if it's underneath a cabinet. It's also easy to put it back on it index is pretty well as it's sliding down and I've had no issues putting the tank back in and having to worry about it not being in right.This is my second purchase of product. I had the first coffee maker only 11mo. It quit pumping water, possibly blocked. I followed all the directions for this issue but no luck. Since under warranty didn’t want to deal with customer service. I ordered a new one. Figured it paid for itself as I never go out for coffee unless social gathering. The first one did have some trouble with latching shut. Only use the self filling pod, never used a commercial pod. Someday I’ll take a part the old coffee maker to diagnose what went wrong?No, I do not work for Cuisinart. I got this coffee maker after hesitating because it said that the item gets returned often. So I almost didn't get it. But I did because Cuisinart makes good quality equipment. Man, I love it. It works so well!!! I am not easily impressed by coffee machines, either.

This is so easy to use and so fast! Such good coffee! So flexible, too. I don't use the pods, but you can use those, too. No issues, have had it for a week. I made 90 weight by using my Cuisinart grinder on fine and stuffed it into the pod. No problems, just great coffee.

I take coffee seriously. I buy it usually 100 pounds at a time green and roast. So when I say it's a good coffee maker, you know it's a good coffee maker.Love this! Fresh ground coffee by the cup! What could be better first thing in the morning? If I had a neg. thing to say is I wish it would make a larger cup of coffee.I have always loved Luerig but this feature of grinding beans makes the coffee taste so much better. Also can do ground coffee or pods.We have had this coffee maker for little over a year now. We have never had a problem with it. It makes a great cup of coffee however you choose to make it. I prefer the already ground coffee and my husband likes to grind his own. It lets you know when it needs cleaned and when the water is too low. No complaints - would buy again!Like easy to use - very straight forward, only 1-2 buttons to push- just want a cup of coffee not play with a computerVery nice item, you can tell it is well made. Makes 3 sizes (8 oz, 10oz, and 12oz). I've been using pre-ground coffee in a Mr Coffee maker. I'm amazed at how much better the fresh ground beans taste. Of course, you can still use pre-ground or the k-cups. But I chose this one because I hate the taste of the plastic k-cups. Fresh ground is the way to go. I wish the water tank was a little larger but, overall, this is a great purchase.
reviews_1s:
I purchased The Cuisinart Single Coffee Maked on Dec1, 2024.....brand spanking new
I am quite disappointed in the Cuisinart Coffee Maker as it does not live up to its quality image
The brewer is incapable of brewing the correct amount of coffee as it apparently is not able to brew a 8, 10, 12 oz cup of coffee.
I press for 8oz and I get 4oz
I press for 10oz and I get 8oz sometimes and sometimes between 6 & 8 oz cup of coffee
I press for 12oz and I get between 8 & 10 oz cup of coffee
I had followed all the directions for 1st time set up and running water filter, cup brewing etc
The grinder is a lot noisier than my regular grinder
The coffee filter is narrow and long and thus paper filters does not fit properly
I have tried coffee filter both with and without paper filters and it constantly spits out more than a few grinded coffee...about a tablespoon+
I bought the Cuisinart Coffee Brewer because image of quality and construction....it's actually a terrible coffee brewer...
I had originally planned to purchase both the single brew and a full carafe brewer and now I have decided not to purchase the full carafe brewer....and I will review other brands to purchase...
Definitely it is a "NO" recommendation for this CuisinartWas extremely pleased at the function of this machine, but that changed quickly. Started to notice that the machine was only producing about 6 oz of coffee from time to time and would have to re-run the brew. The pod holder would slightly pop open during the brew cycle which would stop the machine from brewing. I used a large rubber band and would slide that over the outside pod holder and this stopped the problem ($200 machine with a rubber band holding it together is a bit ridiculous but ok), then it began having issues where the buttons would not register you pushing them. I found if you unplugged and plugged it back in a few times it would reset. I reached out to Cuisinart a total of 5 times with no remedy. On one call I was told to call another day because the rep was “swamped” (wish I was joking!), one rep told me he was having a system outage and would call me back (7 months and still awaiting his call), one rep said she would send me a return label and once they got the machine they would review to see if I DESERVED a replacement (never got the label), upon calling back in to get that label I was told they don’t do replacements and was disconnected, next rep again said they would send a label for return and to expect a replacement in 10-14 days once they received the unit (not sure how you make coffee for a month without a machine… but ok) but again the label never arrived by email, mail or carrier pigeon! I finally gave up!

In short: this coffee machine is great in the beginning, but not durable! Cuisinart customer service is not helpful or customer friendly. In my opinion; save yourself the hassle and pick another machine/brand!I actually really liked the coffee, and as expected a lot of the negative reviews are just super neurotic folks that get bent about have a few grinds fall out of the grinder. So what, clean it up! Its not like using a regular grinder is devoid of any grounds getting on your counter top ....
The issue arose after only about 4 months of use. Delivered volume started to get smaller and smaller and then poof, it just stopped working. Makes terrible noises then all the lights flash and it won't work. No, it's not a decalcification issue. I've had a Mr. Coffee for 20 years. I finally broke down and invested in what I thought was a quality coffee maker. I'm super disappointed and will now start the journey of trying to get a replacement or some sort of refund ... I'll report back. Wish me luck!I have had this coffee machine for about two months. It was inconvenient at first, but I thought I would get used to it. Eventually, I ended up buying a grinder and another (cheaper) drip coffee machine that works just as fine.

Here are the main flaws.

1) The grinder is very messy - like really messy. Every time I used the machine, the grinder would just leave coffee grinds all around. I tried buying another pod so I could immediately replace the one in the machine so it could catch leftovers. That helped a bit, but somehow my kitchen counter always had coffee crumbles around.

2) The water container is small. If you drink a lot of coffee (or if more than one person will be using this machine) you will have to refill it very often.

3) The grinder gets clogged easily and is hard to clean. For some reason, the container for the coffee beans is fixed to the machine, so every time it gets clogged, cleaning requires a lot of effort.

4) Flavor is only marginally better than regular drip coffee - and not worth all the effort that goes into the constant cleaning and having to prepare coffee cups one by one.

I know that the idea of using freshly ground coffee sounds nice. That is why I bought this machine, but do not get seduced by it! Just get yourself a grinder and a better coffee maker.

One good thing I can say about it: as a kitchen appliance, it looks nice.From the first use, (used only twice) brew drips all over the place! Does not come down in a straight line. Made sure no clogged holes, but still messy dripping. Eventually had to call their customer service, because the online customer service area said "Not available". After waiting for a long time, a customer service guy asked for the model number AND the serial number!! Gave him the serial number, but he said he can't help unless he has the model number too! Well, that number in apparently ON THE BOTTOM of the machine ! I have the water reservoir full of water, and would have to maybe take that off, empty the bean holder which has beans in it, and all the other removable parts in order to turn over the machine ! I was so angry I told him to forget it, and that the machine is a piece of junk, and hung up !! Bottom line I do NOT recommend this machine, and their customer service is useless. Mind you, I had already done the online product registration too. I am too angry to go through all that's needed to get to the model number. If they knew that number would be required, why didn't they put it on the back of the machine??? Guess, serial number and model number are very different. On the user manual, it has the Series number on it, but they need more. I think they put that other number on the bottom, hoping to discourage us from ever trying to get any kind of service ! They need to just send me a new replacement !! Then I would update this horrid review !During the past 2 weeks we have purchased 5 Grind and Brew Single coffee makers, 2 from our local Bed Bath and Beyond and 2 from Kohls (on line) and one through Amazon. NONE of the units arrived operational. They all had the same problem. We followed the initial setup instructions exactly as shows in the instructions manual. When the power button was pushed, the power came on BUT the 6, 8, and 12oz selection button would not. The grinder worked,,,, but you could not select a brew option. When we took each of the three units back to Bed Bath and Beyond, the very helpful manage tried to get the units to work, no success. The last unit we return, another customer was in front of us returning the same unit because of the same issue. It was her second unit. She had an earlier version of the same unit and LOVED it. The manager could only assume there was a batch problem with the mother board. With that as a possibility, we tried ordering from a different store hoping perhaps they would be from a different batch and perhaps have different results. They still would not power the brew buttons. I am taking these back tomorrow. We still want this coffeemaker-- but we do not know where to get one that will work. I am puzzled this problem exists because in each coffeemaker, there is a slip of paper stating "This" coffeemaker was tasted with coffee at the factory. This seems unlikely with so many units not working. Frustrating is an understatement. We are trying one more time and have ordered on directly from Cuisinart. Last attempt. Waiting and hoping.11/18/2024 K-cups kept exploding when trying to make coffee. Couldn't clean up the grounds properly after exploding. Coffee continued to drip after removing cup/mug and coffee grounds continued to drop after reusable filter cup was removed from under the grinder. Water reservoir and filter turned green. It was Impossible to clean the water level indicator of the green. I returned this coffee maker purchased 6/2024 to Cuisinart with a letter and photos of the problems requesting a refund in 8/2024. I did NOT want a replacement. This coffee maker is a health hazard. Never heard from Cuisinart. I used to like Cuisinart products! NO STARS (had to choose 1) for this Cuisinart DGB-2W Grind and Brew Single -Serve Coffeemaker.Warning: Cuisinart WILL NOT issue a refund if purchased through Amazon (will only offer a letter of credit). My Grind and Brew had unusual water leaking at exactly one year, then started popping and exploding K cups. Cuisinart sent a replacement and it exploded (grinds flying out of unit and hitting wall) at exactly 30 days of use. I spent a month pleading my case with Cuisinart and requesting a full refund - "We don't do that," said every "supervisor" I spoke with. I hope I just had a rare, defective product but I see Amazon now has this as a frequently returned item.THIS IS THE MESSIEST WORST K CUP DEVICE I HAVE EVER USED. IT IS ABSOLUTELY AWFUL. DO NOT BUY THIS, GET A DIFFERENT BRAND. HORRIBLE. HORRIBLE. HORRIBLE. I WOULD GET IT NEGATIVE STARS IF POSSIBLE. THE K CUP OPTION BLOWS UP AND RARELY WORKS THE GRINDER SPURTS GROUNDS EVERYWHERE. HALF THE TIME THE COFFEE BREWS ONLY A SMALL PORTION OF WHAT IT SHOULD AND THEN THE NEXT IT IS FINE. YOU ARE CONSTANTLY EATING COFFEE GROUNDS. IT IS NOT GOODMy experience with this coffee maker started off well enough. For the first two months, it worked fine—grinding the coffee, then stopping to brew it. However, by the third month, it wouldn't stop grinding. Despite cleaning it and trying again, the problem persisted. By the time I realized this, it was too late to return it. I had read the reviews and decided to take a chance, but unfortunately, it did not pay off. With no option for a refund, I ended up having to throw it away.
